events.event_description: Henry Augustus Philip Muhlenberg presents credentials as the first Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary to Austria, establishing a U.S. legation.

evidence.quote: Henry Augustus Philip Muhlenberg, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1838-02-08. Credentials presented: 1838-11-07. Left post on 1840-09-18.

events.event_description: John Lothrop Motley presents credentials as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, elevating the U.S. mission from a legation to an embassy.

evidence.quote: John Lothrop Motley, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1861-08-10. Credentials presented: 1861-11-14. Left post: 1867-06-14.

events.event_description: Henry Miller Watts presents credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, reverting the U.S. mission from embassy to legation status.

evidence.quote: Henry Miller Watts, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1868-07-25. Credentials presented: 1868-09-25. Presented recall on 1869-06-01.

events.event_description: Robert Sanderson McCormick is promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary, elevating the U.S. mission from legation to embassy.

evidence.quote: Robert Sanderson Mccormick, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1901-03-07. Credentials presented: 1901-04-29. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary on 1902-06-26.

events.event_description: Austria-Hungary severs relations with the United States, ending all formal U.S. diplomatic representation.

evidence.quote: Joseph Clark Grew, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Credentials presented: 1913-04-07. Austria-Hungary severed relations with the United States on 1917-04-09.

events.event_description: Arthur Hugh Frazier is received as Chargé d'Affaires pro tempore on an informal, provisional basis without a formal commission, establishing a liaison-level presence.

evidence.quote: Arthur Hugh Frazier, Chargé d'Affaires pro tempore. Credentials presented: 1921-11-26. Left post on 1922-05-21. Note: Not commissioned; letter of credence dated November 25, 1921. Recognized provisionally as Charge d'Affaires pro tempore and received as such on November 26, 1921.

events.event_description: Albert Henry Washburn presents credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, re-establishing a formal U.S. legation in Austria.

evidence.quote: Albert Henry Washburn, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1922-02-10. Credentials presented: 1922-06-19. Died at post on 1930-04-29.

events.event_description: The U.S. legation in Austria is closed following Austria's annexation by Germany on March 13, 1938, ending all formal U.S. diplomatic representation.

evidence.quote: John Cooper Wiley, Chargé d'Affaires ad interim. Credentials presented: 1937-09-26. Legation closed on 1938-04-30. Note: Austria was annexed to Germany on March 13, 1938.

events.event_description: John George Erhardt presents credentials as Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary, re-establishing a U.S. legation in post-war Austria.

evidence.quote: John George Erhardt, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1946-08-03. Credentials presented: 1946-09-07. Left post on 1950-06-27.

events.event_description: Walter Joseph Donnelly is promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary and presents credentials, elevating the U.S. mission from legation to embassy.

evidence.quote: Walter Joseph Donnelly, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1950-09-20. Credentials presented: 1950-10-25. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary on 1951-11-28.
